commands.review_summary:
The branch adds pre-normalization runtime tool schema quarantine across agent runtime, compaction, doctor reporting, and Codex dynamic tool registration, with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +280, Tests +344. Total +624 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: current main reads tool entries, names, and parameters before guarded quarantine, and the PR adds proxy/getter tests for those cases. I did not run a live failing turn in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the compatibility tradeoff and normal landing gates.

target.title: fix(agents): harden runtime tool schema quarantine
target.body:
## Summary

- quarantines unreadable, non-object, malformed, or non-serializable runtime tool schemas before RuntimePlan/provider normalization can crash an assistant or compaction turn
- preserves metadata only from surviving normalized tools, so quarantined tools are not re-read during metadata copying or diagnostic emission
- surfaces pre-normalization diagnostics through runtime logging, compaction, doctor warnings/findings, and Codex dynamic tool build logging while intentionally dropping the older effective-inventory expansion from this landing slice

This is the pruned critical hardening PR. The paused stacked doctor PR should stay parked unless a smaller follow-up has a concrete missing CLI/reporting behavior after this lands.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/tools.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/active-tool-schema-warnings.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed 5 shards / 83 tests after the final rebase
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/active-tool-schema-warnings.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/active-tool-schema-warnings.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/tools.test.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/tools.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts` passed
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed
- autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings, overall `patch is correct (0.83)`

Remote changed-gate note: fresh remote `pnpm check:changed` is still not claimed for this SHA. The known runner blockers from this workstream are Blacksmith Testbox auth missing, Azure lease timeout, and AWS Crabbox monthly budget exhaustion.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: unsupported or unreadable active runtime tool schemas are quarantined before runtime/provider normalization can crash an assistant, compaction, doctor, or Codex dynamic tool build path.

Real environment tested: local targeted runtime, doctor, and Codex app-server tests on PR head `66ffc7a271fd97657dc2d7b0495373fc411ca374`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the focused Vitest command, targeted oxlint command, `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`, and autoreview commands listed above after rebasing the replacement branch.

Evidence after fix: focused tests passed, oxlint/diff checks passed, and autoreview returned no actionable findings after verifying the earlier review holes were fixed.

Observed result after fix: bad tool entries are removed before name-based Codex filtering and provider normalization; doctor and quarantine logging use guarded reads so unreadable entries produce diagnostics instead of crashing the reporting path.

What was not tested: fresh remote changed gate on the final SHA because the available remote runners were blocked as noted above.
